1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Ruin is for every open slanderer, backbiter.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Woe to every slanderer and backbiter.

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Woe to every scandal-monger and slanderer,

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: Woe to every scorner and mocker

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: Woe to every slanderer and backbiter

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Woe to every (kind of) scandal-monger and-backbiter,

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: Who amasses wealth and hordes it.

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: Who accumulated wealth and hoarded it, counting.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: who has gathered riches and counted them over

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Who amasseth wealth; and counteth it.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Who has gathered wealth and counted it,

2.author: Itani
2.text: Who gathers wealth and counts it over.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: who amasses wealth and counts it over and again.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: Who has gathered wealth and counted it.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: Who hath gathered wealth (of this world) and arranged it.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: who amasses wealth and counts it over.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: who amasses wealth and counts it,

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: Who collects wealth and [continuously] counts it.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: Who amasses wealth and considers it a provision (against mishap);

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: who amasses wealth, counting it over,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: Who pileth up wealth and layeth it by,

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Does he think his wealth will abide for ever with him?

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Does he think that his wealth will prolong his stay on earth forever?

3.author: Arberry
3.text: thinking his riches have made him immortal!

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: He bethinketh that his wealth shall abide for him.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: He thinks that his wealth will make him last forever!

3.author: Itani
3.text: Thinking that his wealth has made him immortal.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: He thinks that his wealth will immortalise him forever.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: He thinks that his wealth will make him last forever!

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: He thinketh that his wealth will render him immortal.

3.author: Qarai
3.text: He supposes his wealth will make him immortal!

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: thinking his wealth will render him immortal!

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: He thinks that his wealth will make him immortal.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: thinking that his property will make him live forever.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: He thinks that his wealth will make him immortal.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: thinking that his wealth will make him live forever.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Thinking that his wealth would make him last for ever!

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: By no means. He will be thrown into Hutama.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: Never! He will certainly be thrown into the Crushing One.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: No indeed; he shall be thrust into the Crusher;

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: By no means! He shall surely be cast into the Crushing Fire.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Nay! Verily, he will be thrown into the crushing Fire.

4.author: Itani
4.text: By no means. He will be thrown into the Crusher.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: Nay, he shall be thrown into the Crusher.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: But no! Verily, he will be thrown into Al-Hutamah.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: Nay, but verily he will be flung to the Consuming One.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: No indeed! He will surely be cast into the Crusher.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: On the contrary! He shall be flung to the Crusher.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: No! He will surely be thrown into the Crusher.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: By no means! They will be thrown into hutamah.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Nay! he shall most certainly be hurled into the crushing disaster,

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: By no means! He shall surely be cast into the crushing torment.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: By no means! He will be sure to be thrown into That which Breaks to Pieces,

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: How will you comprehend what Hutama is?

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: And what have you understood what the Crushing One is!

5.author: Arberry
5.text: and what shall teach thee what is the Crusher;

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: And what shall make thee know that which the Crushing Fire is?

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: And what will make you know what the crushing Fire is?

5.author: Itani
5.text: And what will make you realize what the Crusher is?

5.author: Maududi
5.text: And what do you know what the Crusher is?

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: And what will make you know what Al-Hutamah is

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Ah, what will convey unto thee what the Consuming One is!

5.author: Qarai
5.text: And what will show you what is the Crusher?

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: What shall let you know what the Crusher is?

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: And what can make you know what is the Crusher?

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: Would that you knew what hutamah is!

5.author: Shakir
5.text: And what will make you realize what the crushing disaster is?

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: Would that you understood what that crushing torment is like.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: And what will explain to thee That which Breaks to Pieces?

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: It is the fire kindled by God

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: The fire of Allah, that is ablaze.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: It is the Fire kindled by Allah,

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: (It is) the fire of Allah, kindled,

6.author: Qarai
6.text: [It is] the fire of Allah, set ablaze,

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: (It is) the kindled Fire of Allah,

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: It is the fire of Allah, [eternally] fueled,

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: It is a fierce fire created by God

6.author: Shakir
6.text: It is the fire kindled by Allah,

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: It is a Fire kindled by God.

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: (It is) the Fire of (the Wrath of) Allah kindled (to a blaze),

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: Which will climb on to the hearts.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Which mounteth up to the hearts.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: Which leaps up over the hearts,

7.author: Maududi
7.text: the Fire that shall rise to the hearts (of criminals).

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: Which leaps up over the hearts.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Which leapeth up over the hearts (of men).

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: Which mounts directed at the hearts.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: Reaching right into the hearts of men,

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: The which doth mount (Right) to the Hearts:

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: Indeed it will be shut over them.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Verily it shall close upon them,

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Verily, it shall be closed in on them,

8.author: Maududi
8.text: Verily it will close in upon them,

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Verily, it shall Mu'sadah upon them,

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Lo! it is closed in on them

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Indeed it will close in upon them

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Indeed, Hellfire will be closed down upon them

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Surely it shall be closed over upon them,

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: it closes in on them from every side

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: It shall be made into a vault over them,

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: In pillars stretched forth (i.e. they will be punished in the Fire with pillars, etc.).

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: in its long columns of flames.
